text:
*[Bug 19016] New: as.numeric("NA") throws an unnecessary coercion warning*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19016>

            Bug ID: 19016
           Summary: as.numeric("NA") throws an unnecessary coercion
                    warning
           Product: R
           Version: R 4.5.x
          Hardware: All
                OS: All
            Status: UNCONFIRMED
          Severity: minor
          Priority: P5
         Component: Wishlist
          Assignee: <mailto:R-core@R-project.org|R-core@R-project.org>
          Reporter: <mailto:dan.chaltiel@gmail.com|dan.chaltiel@gmail.com>

Coercing the string "NA" to numeric produces a warning:

> as.numeric("NA")
[1] NA
Warning message:
NAs introduced by coercion

However, the returned value is already the correct numeric result (NA).
The warning is triggered because the coercion fails rather than because
the value itself is problematic.

In practice, this warning is not very informative, since the result is
already the expected one. 

For comparison, other special numeric strings are accepted silently:

> as.numeric("NaN")
[1] NaN
> as.numeric("Inf")
[1] Inf

This can generate unnecessary warnings when converting character data
that contains the common textual representation "NA" for missing values.
